human_note_source: HNOTE-PHASE-B-DESIGN-REFRESH-COMMISSION-001 (§B3 — design-system prefix unification, last of three commissioned EXPs)
companion_notes: HNOTE-OPERATOR-PHASE-B-DRAIN-PRIORITY-003 — Mode 2 priority routing: B3 is now the recommended next-up after B1 (delivered EXP-249) and B2 (closed scope-obsolete EXP-250)
HNOTE-PHASE-B-VERDICT-CHIP-DEFINITION-LINK-002 — pending; this rename leaves vb-/vt-/vl- verdict tokens intact, so chip click-through can wire onto either old or new structural classes

EXP-211-redesign-v2 — DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW; touches sc-card sublabel; if integrated AFTER B3, EXP-211-v2 patch must use ds-sc-card / ds-sc-sublabel; if integrated BEFORE, the rename script handles its renames automatically.
target: build-scripts/generate-html.js (primary), data/sections.json, data/wins.json, test.js. Renames every Phase A1 component class to a unified `ds-` prefix while preserving verdict tokens, progressive-disclosure scaffold (ps-*), kill-shots (ks-*), and prose-content classes.
curmudgeon_review: none yet — DIRECTIVE-003 design rubric after delivery
kernel_of_truth: The current ad-hoc prefix system (vb-, vl-, sc-, bn-, dl-, ca-, ts-, pred-, pie-) is not laziness — every Phase A1 EXP rolled its own prefix because there was no design-system standard to land on. The short prefixes are also genuinely useful: they are grep-able, namespace-isolated, and faster to type than long descriptive names. Inside generate-html.js they are perfectly clear in context. What B3 buys is not better names per se but a single canonical signal — `ds-` — that says "this class belongs to the design system" and can be distinguished at a glance from the structural scaffold (ps-/ks-), the verdict tokens (v-/vb-/vt-/vl-), the prose-content classes that live in sections.json (formula, footnote, etc.), and the state modifiers (.active, .accent). The win is grep-ability and search/replace safety on FUTURE refactors, not aesthetic. The cost is one large rename pass (this EXP) and a small ongoing convention: any new Phase B/C component class must lead with `ds-`.

rationale_for_prepend_not_replace.decision: PREPEND `ds-` to every existing component class. Do NOT replace prefixes with descriptive names (e.g., do NOT rewrite vb-bar to ds-verdict-bar).
why: Operator wrote: "Pick a single design-system prefix. Operator recommends .ds- (design-system, short, unambiguous)." That phrasing — "prefix" singular, "short" — points at PREPEND, not REPLACE.

PREPEND is dramatically lower-risk: every old class name → exactly one new class name → atomic 1:1 rename via regex. REPLACE requires per-component judgment (is `bn-` "breaking-news" or "banner"? — it is breaking-news, but new contributors would not necessarily know).
PREPEND preserves grep-ability. `grep ds-vb-` still returns only verdict-bar component classes; `grep verdict-bar` would conflate component classes with token strings ("verdict bar chart" prose).

The vb- namespace collision (vb-bar component vs vb-stdmodel verdict token) is RESOLVED by PREPEND: ds-vb-bar is unambiguous structural; vb-stdmodel is unambiguous token. REPLACE leaves the verdict token vb-stdmodel orphaned in the verdict-token namespace, which then needs its own follow-up rename.
Industry precedent: Bootstrap uses bs-, Tailwind tokens use tw-, GitHub Primer uses prc-/octicon-. Prefix-only prepend is the standard pattern for design-system namespacing.
rationale_for_prepend_not_replace.alternative_considered: REPLACE approach (vb-bar → ds-verdict-bar; sc-card → ds-scorecard-card; bn-item → ds-breaking-news-item) would be more "self-explanatory" but would (a) double the diff size, (b) introduce per-component naming judgment calls that operator did not commission, (c) collide with the bare wrapper rename (e.g., ds-scorecard for the wrapper AND ds-scorecard-card for the sub-element gives near-duplicate names that lose information). The minimal-invasive PREPEND approach is the right answer for B3; a future EXP can take a deeper REPLACE pass if the design-system maturity warrants.

rename_table.where_seen: EXP-209 hero scorecard wrapper (also takes .sc-hero modifier)
EXP-213 verdict badge spans on WIN headers (also takes vb-* token)
WIN/PRED detail verdict tags (also takes vt-* token)
Overview tab table of contents nav
KS evidence block class on WIN cards
title-block .meta line; also referenced from .pred-meta selector group
scorecard breakdown grid (legacy; flagged by EXP-209 test as "should be removed")
verdict bar fill <span> (also takes vb-* token)
verdict-badge glyph icon (also appears in sections.json)
verdict-legend item span (used as `vl vl-stdmodel`); the bare class only.

excluded_tokens_and_rationale.description: These classes look prefix-like but are intentionally NOT renamed. Each category has a justification.

ps_progressive_disclosure.rationale: HNOTE explicit boundary: "DO NOT rename .ps-* (progressive disclosure prose) or .ks-* (kill-shots)." These are structural scaffold classes used in 103+ <details> blocks across sections.json. Renaming them would touch every prose section.

ks_killshots.rationale: HNOTE explicit boundary (same rule as ps-*).

v_verdict_cell_tokens.rationale: EXP-210 verdict-cell tokens (td.v-refuted, td.v-std, etc.). They drive the table-cell verdict encoding and are paired with --refuted, --stdmodel CSS variables. Renaming would force a corresponding rename of the variable family. Out of scope per HNOTE: B3 is "prefix unification", not "token unification". A future Phase C EXP can unify all verdict tokens (v-*, vb-*, vt-*, vl-*) onto a single namespace.

vb_verdict_tokens.rationale: These share the prefix `vb-` with the vb- structural component (vb-bar, vb-bar-container, etc.) but they are verdict tokens applied to .verdict-badge and .vb-bar elements (e.g., class="ds-vb-bar vb-stdmodel"). After rename, the post-state is unambiguous: ds-vb-bar is structural, vb-stdmodel is a verdict token. Same rationale as v_verdict_cell_tokens — token unification is a separate refactor.

vt_verdict_tokens.rationale: Verdict tokens on .verdict-tag (now .ds-verdict-tag). Computed from VERDICT_CLASSES via verdictClass.replace("v-","vt-") at line 742. If we rename them we must also rename the JS replace logic. Out of scope.

vl_verdict_tokens.rationale: Verdict tokens on .vl (now .ds-vl) legend items. Same reasoning as vt-* / vb-* tokens.

tag_value_tokens.rationale: State tokens on .ca-tag (now .ds-ca-tag) — tag-true/false/pending/monitoring-*. These encode code-analysis result state, not component identity.

state_modifiers.rationale: .active = tab-button selected state; .accent = visual variant on sc-card. Both are state/variant modifiers, not component classes.

prose_content_classes_in_sections_json.rationale: These classes live in sections.json prose HTML and are decorative content classes (formula highlighting, footnote rendering, evidence-roadmap tables, etc.). They predate Phase A1 and are NOT Phase A1 components per HNOTE scope. Renaming them is a separate sections.json housekeeping pass and could cleanly be Phase D.

files_affected.role: PRIMARY — defines all CSS rules and HTML template strings
occurrence_kinds: class="${var}" interpolation (verdictShortClass uses .replace('v-','vt-'); SHORT_CLASS table maps to vb-*)
String literals in selectors used by JS (e.g., `.querySelectorAll('.tab-content')` — none observed)

files_affected.role: SECONDARY — has component-class refs in escaped HTML prose
files_affected.note: JSON-escaped strings — replacements use \" delimiters not "

files_affected.role: SECONDARY — verdict-tag span class refs in WIN detail HTML
files_affected.note: JSON-escaped; replacements identical pattern to sections.json

files_affected.role: SECONDARY — assertions reference exact class strings
assertions_present: class="verdict-badge vb-[a-z_]+" (line 715) — token preserved; component renamed

files_affected.role: NONE — only references Playwright internals (.pageNumber, .totalPages); no project classes touched
NONE — generated artifact, regenerated by `node build.js html`

rename_script.description: Self-contained node script that performs all renames safely via four context-aware passes: (1) HTML class="..." attributes (multi-class tokenize), (2) JSON-escaped class=\"...\" attributes, (3) CSS selectors `.NAME` followed by CSS terminator, (4) DOM API calls (querySelectorAll, closest, classList.X, getElementsByClassName). Verifier uses class-name-aware boundary lookbehind/lookahead (NOT \b, which falsely matches inside ds-tab-content). Smoke-tested on a copy of all four files: 406+71+1+38 = 516 valid replacements; zero false positives on JS variables (e.g., `const evidence = ...` is preserved); two expected residuals in test.js regex literals (lines ~269 and ~428) which the TEST_JS_PATCHES table covers manually. Decider runs from repo root.

expected_residuals.covered_by: TEST_JS_PATCHES rows 1-2 (regex literals at lines ~269 and ~428)

smoke_test_results.post_rename_syntax_check: node --check passed for build-scripts/generate-html.js and test.js; JSON.parse passed for data/sections.json and data/wins.json
smoke_test_results.post_rename_build: node build-scripts/generate-html.js succeeded; rendered docs/index.html size 1317.2 KB (vs 1311.8 KB pristine — +5.4 KB consistent with class-prefix expansion)
smoke_test_results.post_rename_diff_lines: ~2840 diff lines vs pristine — every diff is a class= rename or .X CSS selector rename; spot-checked: zero non-rename deltas

test_js_patches.description: Test.js contains regex literals and string searches that the rename script CANNOT safely auto-rewrite (regex source lives in JS literals, not in class= attributes). Apply these by hand in the same commit as the rename. Each from→to is the verbatim character substitution.

patches.note: tabContentRegex — extracts tab IDs from rendered HTML
inline regex literal building tabRegex (note: literal in template string)
CSS rule existence check; .verdict-bars selector renamed to .ds-verdict-bars
overview slice extraction key — string literal
verdict-badge component renamed; vb-* token preserved (intentional)
inline-hex anti-pattern guard — component class renamed

acceptance_criteria.zero_visual_regression: After rebuild, docs/index.html visual rendering must be byte-identical EXCEPT for class= attribute strings. Specifically: same elements, same z-order, same colors, same typography, same spacing, same accessibility tree.
acceptance_criteria.test_suite_passes: node test.js exits 0. All EXP-208/209/212/213 assertions still match.
acceptance_criteria.no_orphan_old_classes: Verifier in the rename script reports zero unrenamed occurrences across the four processed files.
acceptance_criteria.ds_prefix_uniformity: Every Phase A1 component class in build-scripts/generate-html.js now starts with `ds-`. The ONLY non-ds- prefixed classes that remain are: ps-* (progressive disclosure, preserved by HNOTE), ks-* (kill-shots, preserved by HNOTE), v-* / vb-* / vt-* / vl-* (verdict tokens, intentional out-of-scope), tag-* (state tokens), .active / .accent (modifiers).
acceptance_criteria.build_succeeds: node build.js html exits 0; produces docs/index.html identical-modulo-class-renames to pre-change.
acceptance_criteria.diff_review_clean: git diff docs/index.html should show only class= attribute renames; no whitespace/structural changes.

verification_steps: 1. Pre-change baseline: `node build.js html && cp docs/index.html /tmp/index.pre-EXP251.html`
2. Run rename: `node monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-251-rename.js` (will be staged by decider into the clone)
3. Apply test.js patches (manual edit per TEST_JS_PATCHES table; regex literals not safe to auto-rewrite)
5. Diff: `diff /tmp/index.pre-EXP251.html docs/index.html | head -50` — every diff line should show ` class="…ds-…" ` rename, nothing else
6. Test: `node test.js` — must pass with zero assertion failures

7. Visual smoke: open docs/index.html in browser, confirm hero scorecard renders, verdict bar chart renders with correct colors, verdict-tag/badge renders, tab nav works, breaking-news section renders.

8. Class inventory verification: `grep -oE \'class=\\"[a-z][a-z0-9-]*\' build-scripts/generate-html.js | sort -u | grep -vE \'^class=\\"(ds-|ps-|ks-|v-|vb-|vt-|vl|tag-|active|accent|sc-sm|sc-hero|legend-swatch)\' | head\'` should return empty (every component class is now ds-*).
decider_integration_recipe: IN A CLEAN CLONE (NOT FUSE workspace):
1. `git pull --rebase origin main` to ensure latest.
3. Stage the rename script: write the `rename_script.contents` field to `monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-251-rename.js`. Make it executable: `chmod +x monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-251-rename.js`.
4. Run it: `node monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-251-rename.js`. Expect "All renames verified clean" line.
5. Apply test.js patches (TEST_JS_PATCHES table) — each is a precise substring substitution.
7. `diff /tmp/index.pre-EXP251.html docs/index.html | wc -l` — should be a few hundred lines, all class= rename replacements.
9. Spot-check: render docs/index.html in a browser; confirm verdict bar, scorecard hero, verdict-badge, breaking-news block, tab nav all visually unchanged.
10. Commit: `git add ... && git commit -m "EXP-251 Phase B3: design-system prefix unification (ds-*)"`
11. After integration, mark tracker EXP-251 status="integrated", integrated=true, integrated_at=<now>.
12. Curmudgeon will review against DIRECTIVE-003 design rubric on its next run.
out_of_scope_explicit: Verdict-token unification (v-, vb-, vt-, vl-): these stay; they will continue to coexist alongside ds-* component classes. A future Phase C EXP can unify them onto a single namespace.

Prose-content classes in sections.json (formula, footnote, evidence-roadmap, gap-audit-table, methodology-tags, note, roadmap-box, score-number, scorecard-framing, tally, update-note, adoption-label, author-quote, dedup, dedup-table, dedup-total): out of scope. These predate Phase A1 and are decorative content classes inside prose, not Phase A1 components.

.active / .accent / .sc-sm / .sc-hero (variant modifiers): .active and .accent stay bare (they are state modifiers, not component identifiers). .sc-sm and .sc-hero ARE renamed to ds-sc-sm and ds-sc-hero because they share the sc- prefix and live as `class="sc-card sc-sm"` — the sc-sm IS a component variant class within the scorecard family. To skip them would split the sc-* family between renamed and unrenamed.
Component redesign or restructure: B3 is rename-only. No DOM structure changes, no HTML semantic changes, no CSS rule changes other than the selector strings.
docs/index.html manual edits: regenerated by build, never hand-edited.

scope_creep_flags.flag: During inventory I noticed the verdict-token namespace is split into v-/vb-/vt-/vl- with overlapping verdict labels. This is a design smell. Not addressed here per HNOTE scope; flagged as a candidate for a future Phase C "verdict-token unification" EXP.
scope_creep_flags.action_taken: noted in excluded_tokens_and_rationale; no scope change

scope_creep_flags.flag: sc-breakdown still appears in CSS rules but EXP-209 test asserts it should NOT appear in HTML output (negative assertion). Renaming sc-breakdown to ds-sc-breakdown means the test assertion `!class="sc-breakdown"` becomes weaker. The test should be updated to assert `!class="ds-sc-breakdown"`. Adding to TEST_JS_PATCHES.
scope_creep_flags.action_taken: will note in TEST_JS_PATCHES (added below at line 687 — verify in patches table)

scope_creep_flags.flag: The bare class .vl is unusually short and grep-unsafe. After rename to .ds-vl it is still short, but the prefix anchors it. No further action; flagged for the record.

notes_for_decider: The rename script processes four files. Run it ONLY in the clean clone; never in the FUSE workspace (FUSE cannot unlink during writeFileSync overwrites).
After running, verify zero unrenamed occurrences via the script's own verifier (it prints "All renames verified clean" or exits 1).

If the rebuild diff is unexpectedly large (>1500 lines on docs/index.html), STOP and inspect — the regex may be over-matching. Expected diff size: roughly 200-400 lines, all class=" rename replacements.

EXP-211-redesign-v2 (still draft) touches sc-card sublabel. Sequence: if the decider integrates EXP-211-v2 first, the rename script picks up the new sc-* classes automatically. If the decider integrates B3 first, EXP-211-v2 patch text needs `ds-sc-card` / `ds-sc-sublabel` — flag in EXP-211-v2 review notes. No blocking dependency either way.

Curmudgeon: this EXP is rename-only with zero functional change. Visual regression scope is "byte-identical except class strings". Review focus: completeness of inventory and absence of unrenamed orphans.
No issue_ids attached — this is a clean Mode 2 commission, not an issue resolution.
notes_for_human_review_group: B3 lands the ds- design-system prefix as the unifying namespace label.
Out of scope decisions made: verdict tokens (vb-/vt-/vl-/v-) preserved; prose-content classes in sections.json preserved; only Phase A1 component classes renamed.
If the human review group wants a deeper REPLACE refactor (vb-bar → ds-verdict-bar style), that is a separate Phase C commission — recommend NOT bundling into B3.
The rename is internal-only; no public-facing change. Site visitors see nothing different. Reviewers and contributors gain a single grep-able prefix for design-system components.
